title: Your Heart Has Trembled To My Tongue
content:
Your heart has trembled to my tongue,
    Your hands in mine have lain,
    Your thought to me has leaned and clung,
    Again and yet again,
    My dear,
    Again and yet again.

    Now die the dream, or come the wife,
    The past is not in vain,
    For wholly as it was your life
    Can never be again,
    My dear,
    Can never be again.

    1876
meaning:
"Your Heart Has Trembled To My Tongue" is a quintessential example of William Ernest Henley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
